front: Movement: term coined by M.L. Rosenthal in a review of Robert Lowell's Life Studies
Movement: focuses on personal experience, trauma, mental illness
Movement: arguably launched by a poem titled for a remark about "an only daughter" in The Madness of Suibhne, about the poet's separation from daughter Cynthia
back: Confessional poetry (Heart's Needle by W.D. Snodgrass)

front: Movement: landmark collection contains "Skunk Hour" (narrator declares "I myself am Hell"), "Beyond the Alps" (train from Rome to Paris), and "91 Revere Street"
back: Confessional poetry (Life Studies by Robert Lowell)

front: Movement: a poem from this school opens "Stasis in darkness" and declares "White Godiva, I unpeel"
back: Confessional poetry (Ariel / "Lady Lazarus" by Sylvia Plath)

front: Movement: ends with "the red eye, the cauldron of morning"
back: Confessional poetry (Ariel / "Lady Lazarus" by Sylvia Plath)

front: Movement: a poem from this school states suicides "want to know which tools. They never ask why build"
back: Confessional poetry ("Wanting to Die" by Anne Sexton)

front: Movement: from a collection titled for an "awful" action
back: Confessional poetry ("Wanting to Die" by Anne Sexton)
